channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Git Explained Simply — For People Who Have No Idea What It Is
item.description: You've seen the word Git everywhere. Job listings. GitHub. Tutorials that just assume you already know what it means. Nobody ever stopped to explain it from scratch. This post is that explanation....

item.title: Understanding Objects in JavaScript
item.description: Objects are one of the most important concepts in JavaScript. Almost everything in JavaScript eventually revolves around objects. If arrays help us store lists of data, objects help us store...

item.title: I built memory decay for AI agents using the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve
item.description: Most AI memory systems treat every fact equally, forever. That felt wrong to me. If you tell Claude you use React, then six weeks later say you switched to Vue both facts exist in memory with the...

item.title: Trump administration is allegedly collecting $10 billion on the TikTok deal
item.description: In September, Donald Trump claimed that "the United States is getting a tremendous fee" for brokering the TikTok deal. Now sources tell the Wall Street Journal and the New York Times that fee is...

item.title: US Army announces contract with Anduril worth up to $20B
item.description: The Army described this as a single enterprise contract consolidating more than 120 separate "procurement actions."

item.title: Meta is reportedly laying off up to 20 percent of its staff
item.description: According to Reuters, Meta is looking to offset spending on AI and data centers with a massive round of layoffs. Sources familiar with the matter say the company could lay off as much as 20...

item.title: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
item.description: These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

item.title: Prediction markets want the Oscars to be your gateway drug to betting on everything
item.description: Though it was weird to see the Golden Globes partner with Polymarket for its most recent awards show, the collaboration wasn't shocking given the way that people have begun using prediction...

item.title: MacBook Air M5 review: a small update for the ‘just right’ Mac
item.description: Not much has changed in the new MacBook Air for 2026: It now has the M5 chip, Wi-Fi 7, and faster storage starting at 512GB instead of 256GB. It's just as outstanding a computer as last year's...

item.title: A Scavengers Reign artist explores contemplative sci-fi in new comics
item.description: You may recognize Jonathan Djob Nkondo's work from animated projects like the surreal sci-fi series Scavengers Reign or the recent Gorillaz short film The Mountain, The Moon Cave and The Sad God....

item.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
item.description: Learn how to use Spotify, Canva, Figma, Expedia, and other apps directly in ChatGPT.

item.title: Asus’ new open earbuds are a wonderful companion for handheld gaming
item.description: The Cetra Open Wireless aren’t like most other gaming earbuds. They’re large, with an open-style design that rests just outside of your ears, letting game audio mix with sounds in your...

item.title: GlassWorm Supply-Chain Attack Abuses 72 Open VSX Extensions to Target Developers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new iteration of the GlassWorm campaign that they say represents a "significant escalation" in how it propagates through the Open VSX registry. "Instead of...

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra review: show off
item.description: "Someone might be watching everything I'm doing on my screen," I tell myself in public. Even when I'm doing nothing of consequence - just making my little Wordle guesses - there's a sense of...

item.title: 5 great indie games from GDC 2026
item.description: I just got back from the GDC Festival of Gaming, a big industry-focused event in San Francisco that was formerly known as the Game Developers Conference. While the show is mostly about educational...

item.title: ‘Not built right the first time’ — Musk’s xAI is starting over again, again
item.description: The AI lab is revamping its effort to build an AI coding tool, with two new executives joining from Cursor.

item.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
item.description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

item.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
item.description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Increased Squid Population in the Falklands
item.description: Some good news: squid stocks seem to be recovering in the waters off the Falkland Islands. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t...
